rulings.Case Title: TRINITY MANAGEMENT SERVICES, ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 15, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, OFFICE OF THE TREASURER AND TAX COLLECTOR, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO CONTROLLER'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to address in good faith all issues raised in the demurrer. Respondent's request for judicial notice is GRANTED as to the San Francisco parking tax returns, San Francisco Ordinance 206-13, letters to Petitioners' counsel denying refund claims, and S.F. Business & Tax Regulations Code section 6.2-21; 6.7-1; 6.9-1; 6.11-1 through 6.15-3; 602; and 604. (Evid. Code, section 452(d).) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 To Enforce Settlement Of Cases No. Cgc-19?573988 And Cud-19-665547
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 15, 2023 line 2. PLAINTIFF SAN DONG BEST INC., SHUBO WANG NOTICE OF MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6 TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT OF CASES NO. CGC-19-573988 AND CUD-19-665547 Hearing Required. No later than November 15, 2023 at 9:00 a.m. parties to submit a fully executed settlement agreement as directed by Judge Ross on June 13, 2023 at 11:12 a.m. If parties cannot agree on the final language of the settlement agreement, the parties must submit a joint document clearly indicating which parts are agreed upon (by using standard font) and identifying the disputed language by using other fonts (bold, italics, underlined, etc.), by assigning specific font to each party/signatory. The final executed settlement agreement or the joint document described above shall be submitted to department501@sftc.org. Additionally, no later than 9:00 a.m. on November 15, 2023 Plaintiff shall file a Proof of Service re: Notice of Continued earing. (See November 1, 2023 minutes). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. rulings.Case Title: JANEL MITCHELL VS. GWENDOLYN REHLING ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 15, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANT GWENDOLYN REHLING MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of malice oppression or fraud. Motion to Strike as to the request for attorney's fees is moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Opposition is reviewed and stricken as untimely. The Court notes that on page 7 of the opposition P purports to quote paragraph 44 of FAC. The FAC on file with the Court does not include a paragraph 44 that matches the quoted contents, which reference a Defendant "Ethel Creek, LLC," "a GWENDOLYN REHLING, as an employee and managing agent of Defendant ETHEL CREEK, LLC" and a "Plaintiff, an elderly man." Paragraph 44 of the First Amended Complaint filed on September 13, 2023 reads in its entirety: "Defendants knowingly and repeatedly made the representation to Plaintiff that the subject Property was legal as a residential dwelling and the Subject Property was habitable for Plaintiffs." The parties to the action are listed in paragraphs 1 and 2 of the First Amended Complaint as "Plaintiff Janel Mitchell, an individual, is an adult female individual" and "Defendant GWENDOLYN REHLING, an individual, is an adult female individual ." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
